skill.name: database-migration
skill.description: Guides database schema migration workflows for Prisma and Drizzle, including migration generation, deployment, rollback planning, CI checks, and production safety reviews.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and run versioned database schema migrations with Prisma or Drizzle.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged command execution, network, environment access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found the command, URL, and environment matches are documentation examples, not hidden executable code, exfiltration, or prompt injection. The skill remains medium risk because it requests Bash access and teaches state-changing database migration commands that can affect live systems.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Database Migration Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill requests Bash access and includes npm, npx, Prisma, Drizzle, and CI migration commands. These are legitimate examples, but they can modify or reset databases if run against the wrong environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and the skill explicitly allows Bash. The context is legitimate migration guidance, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Database Credential Handling Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill references DATABASE_URL in environment setup, ORM configuration, GitHub Actions secrets, and a local test connection string. This is expected for migration tooling but can expose secrets if copied into unsafe contexts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable usage is explicit and tied to database connections. No evidence shows exfiltration, but secret handling remains a real review concern.

low_findings.title: External URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Prisma, Drizzle, and PlanetScale documentation. No evidence found of network calls, tracking endpoints, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the References section. They are not used in executable code or instructions that send user data.

low_findings.title: Scanner Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are keyword matches in prose, schema examples, and placeholder migration text. No evidence found of cryptographic code or system reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the cited lines are ordinary migration documentation and model fields. They do not implement crypto or host inspection.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing Migration Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents migration deploy, push, and reset operations. These operations should require explicit confirmation, backups, and environment checks before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines identify database-changing operations. The risk depends on execution context, but the operational impact is clear.

content.value_statement: Schema changes can break production when teams rely on manual SQL and unclear rollback plans. This skill guides versioned migrations, reviews, deployment checks, and safer Prisma or Drizzle workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Prisma setup, schema definition, migration generation, deployment, and client regeneration.
Explains Drizzle schema files, configuration, migration generation, push workflows, and studio usage.
Provides migration safety patterns for small changes, nullable columns, defaults, indexes, and column removal.
Outlines rollback planning with Prisma resolution steps and manual down migration files.
Shows CI validation and production deployment checklist items for database migrations.
limitations: Does not inspect a live database unless the user provides access and approves commands.
Does not guarantee zero downtime for every database engine or application design.
Requires human review before running production migration commands.
Examples focus on PostgreSQL, Prisma, Drizzle, and Node.js projects.

use_cases.title: Add a Versioned Schema Change
use_cases.description: Create a small migration plan, generate files, review SQL, and prepare rollback notes before merging.

use_cases.title: Review Production Migration Safety
use_cases.description: Check backups, lock risks, nullable column strategy, index creation, and rollback steps before release.

use_cases.title: Set Up Migration CI
use_cases.description: Add migration validation to pull requests so schema changes run against a test database before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one schema change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Prisma migration that adds one nullable column. Include generation, SQL review, testing, and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Migration for Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before merging a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this migration plan for production risk. Focus on locks, data backfill, rollback, and required deployment order.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Drizzle Migration Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Drizzle migration workflow for our Node.js service. Include schema files, generated migrations, CI validation, and deployment checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Zero Downtime Release Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced production migration with compatibility requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a phased migration plan for a non-null column on a large production table. Include backward-compatible code changes and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: I need to add a required status field to users.
output: Recommended phased plan with a nullable field first.
Second migration to enforce the non-null constraint.

output_examples.input: Our pull request includes a migration that drops a column.
output: Risk review explaining data loss and deployment order.
Verification checklist for application code and backups.

output_examples.input: Set up CI checks for Prisma migrations.
output: Pull request validation flow using a test database.
Dependency installation and migration deployment steps.
Required secret handling and failure checks.

best_practices: Review generated SQL before merging any migration file.
Back up production data and confirm rollback steps before deployment.
Keep migrations small, ordered, and tied to compatible application changes.
anti_patterns: Running manual production ALTER statements without versioned migration files.
Dropping columns before deployed code stops reading them.
Using destructive reset commands outside disposable development databases.

faq.question: Does this skill run migrations automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and commands. Production execution should require explicit user approval and environment verification.

faq.question: Which tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Prisma, Drizzle, PostgreSQL-style migrations, GitHub Actions, and general migration safety practices.

faq.question: Can it help with rollback planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes rollback scripts, Prisma rollback resolution, and recovery planning before deployment.

faq.question: Is it safe for production databases?
faq.answer: It can support production planning, but commands must be reviewed with backups, monitoring, and clear approval.

faq.question: Does it handle secret management?
faq.answer: It shows DATABASE_URL and GitHub Actions secret usage. Users should avoid pasting real credentials into prompts.

faq.question: Can it design zero downtime migrations?
faq.answer: It gives compatible change patterns, but final safety depends on database size, workload, indexes, and application behavior.
